factors.summary: Free cash flow generation decreased due to a $183.6 million decrease in cash from working capital changes.

evidence.text: year was due primarily to a $183.6 million decrease in cash generated from changes within our working capital accounts (discussed below) offset in part by a small increase in net earnings.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decrease in free cash flow indicates a headwind in capital allocation.

factors.summary: The working capital accounts experienced a decrease in cash flows due to timing of cash receipts and payments.

evidence.text: The $183.6 million decrease in cash flows relating to our working capital accounts was due to the timing of cash receipts and payments within our working capital accounts, including an improvement in cash collections from customers, offset in part by an increase in vendor payments.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The decrease is due to timing factors, which are temporary and not indicative of a trend.

factors.summary: Overall demand appears stable with positive outlook in many markets.

evidence.text: The Company continues to have a positive outlook for many of the industry groups and markets in which our clients operate.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive outlook across many markets suggests favorable demand trends.

factors.summary: Backlog is growing at 5.8% year-over-year, indicating increased order intake.

evidence.text: Backlog at December 26, 2014 was $19.1 billion , and represents an increase of 5.8% over backlog at December 27, 2013 .
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growing backlog indicates increased order intake and positive momentum.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in National Government Programs and Infrastructure.

evidence.text: For the three months ended December 26, 2014 , revenues from clients operating in the National Government Programs market increased $158.3 million , or 31.0% , to $668.3 million from $510.0 million for the corresponding period last year. [...] The increase was due primarily to the FNS acquisition completed during the fourth quarter of fiscal 2014.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards higher-margin segments is a positive development for revenue quality.

factors.summary: Organic growth is present but tempered by the impact of acquisitions.

evidence.text: The Company's net earnings for the three months ended December 26, 2014 increased by $6.3 million , or 6.8% , compared to the corresponding period last year. [...] This increase in revenue was due primarily to the SKM and FNS acquisitions.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Growth is present but tempered by acquisition effects, making the trend mixed.

factors.summary: The National Government Programs and Infrastructure markets are growing fastest, while Oil & Gas-Upstream and Mining and Minerals are slowing.

evidence.text: For the three months ended December 26, 2014 , revenues from clients operating in the National Government Programs market increased $158.3 million , or 31.0% , [...] The increase was primarily due to the FNS acquisition completed during the fourth quarter of fiscal 2014. [...] For the three months ended December 26, 2014 , revenues from clients operating in the Infrastructure market increased $94.1 million , or 32.3% , to $385.6 million from $291.5 million for the corresponding period last year. [...] The decrease occurred within our operations in Canada, Europe, and the U.K., and was partially offset by increased activities in the U.S. [...] The decreased revenues in fiscal 2015 were due primarily to the winding down of a project in the U.S. [...] Globally, our clients in these industries continue to behave conservatively when it comes to new spend.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

factors.summary: Gross margins improved slightly, while operating margins saw mixed performance.

evidence.text: As a percentage of revenues, direct costs of contracts for the three months ended December 26, 2014 was 83.7% . This compares to 85.2% for the three months ended December 27, 2013 . [...] The relationship between direct costs of contracts and revenues will fluctuate between reporting periods depending on the mix of business during the reporting periods being compared as well as the level of margins earned from the various types of services provided.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Gross margins improved slightly, but operating margins are mixed, showing no clear trend.

factors.summary: Management expresses confidence through share repurchases and positive outlook statements.

evidence.text: During the three months ended December 26, 2014, the Company repurchased and retired 2.5 million shares of its common stock under its share repurchase program. Total cash spent for the shares repurchased was $113.7 million. [...] The Company continues to have a positive outlook for many of the industry groups and markets in which our clients operate.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management expresses confidence through share repurchases and positive outlook statements.

factors.summary: Management anticipates stable or improving conditions with a projected effective tax rate below 32.0% for fiscal 2015.

evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Anticipation of stable conditions with a projected tax rate below 32.0%, but no explicit positive or negative sentiment expressed beyond that.

factors.summary: The company is sensitive to crude oil prices affecting the Oil & Gas-Upstream market and potential regulatory changes.

evidence.text: As a result, we have seen some project delays and project cancellations by clients operating in this market. [...] We see certain clients deploying more of their capital budgets to sustaining capital-type programs [...] In addition, we believe consolidation among companies in this industry will provide more stable capital spending in the long-term, but such actions may have significant impacts in the short-term. [...] The Company currently anticipates that its effective tax rate for fiscal 2015 will be below 32.0%. [...] Any notable incidents or recalls.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Sensitivity to crude oil prices and regulatory changes presents potential risks and challenges.

factors.summary: Acquisition activity is creating value through increased revenues and market expansion.

evidence.text: In December 2013, the Company acquired all of the outstanding equity interests in SKM. [...] We also completed a number of other acquisitions during fiscal 2014 including Federal Network Services LLC [...] The results of operations of these other acquisitions were not material, individually or in the aggregate, to the Company's consolidated results of operations for fiscal 2014.
evidence.subsection: Table of Contents JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Acquisition activity is creating value through increased revenues and market expansion.
